1:
This is one of the more complex characters. Egotistical and weak-willed at the same time. Egotism implies asserting one's own views, while weakness of will means submitting to another person's opinion. In everyday life they want to appear important, businesslike people. They strive for power or to be occupied with important matters, so as to demonstrate strength of will. Yet a softness is still felt. This character loves to argue, again wishing to prove their intellect, mind, will and authority. The subject of the argument itself is irrelevant.
• A woman with character 1 values strength of character in a man and will demand it from him, and most importantly — she is always testing his resilience by drawing him into arguments. If the man begins to give in to her, it is the beginning of the family's collapse; she will not forgive him for being weak-willed, because she values him precisely for his strength of character. Women with character 1 may draw out vowel sounds when speaking, which sometimes seems like an affected intonation. They compete with other women to prove their superiority.
• A man strives to hold high positions, to own his own business, to live large — such actions are one of the ways to prove his strength of will and importance.
The way to talk with character 1, so that you can win the argument and yet not take the "victory" away from such a person, is simply to remember this sequence of conversation:
1. I agree with you
2. But personally I would do it differently, namely (a brief plan)…
3. But I agree with you. Decide everything yourself…
4. You hold a pause until he proposes
5. His own plan of decisions. Do not be surprised, it will be
6. The very decision you suggested, because the responsibility will fall on you, yet the decision is also yours.
Result: if all goes well, he will credit the decision to himself. If it goes badly, he will blame you. Let this go past your ears.
Also, person "1" is very soft, weak-willed and egotistical; it is hard for them to assert their own "I." These people, as a rule, avoid making decisions, which results in problems in their personal and professional life. I would add that "1" people are complex and contradictory by their very nature. They avoid making any decisions, trying to shift them onto someone else's shoulders. While running from decision-making, they desperately imitate activity and forcefully display their willful qualities. That is precisely why they always enter into an argument over any matter. Lovers of a good argument.
● Downside: They have an acute need for success, striving to show everyone their strength of character; they love to throw dust in people's eyes. They often become egotistical, and in pursuing victory they fail to notice other people with their requests and troubles. They can be cruel.
● The main goal of this character is to be respected, big and successful, to be better than everyone, to constantly prove their greatness.
● The person is subject to others' influence and opinions. Is often self-absorbed — an egotist.
● They may seem like hedgehogs, because inside they are soft people, and they are very afraid of being hurt, so sometimes they hurt others first, releasing their spines or closing off.
● Women of this character are always wildly feminine, graceful and sexual. Very attractive to men, because they always keep their distance.
● They love to emphasize their status and significance through external attributes (big cars, expensive accessories).
● Notable examples: Monica Bellucci, Angelina Jolie, Egor Kreed, Philipp Kirkorov. "1" carries ancestral karma within them. They bear karma reaching back from the 4th generation. The person must learn to say no and accept the fact that they are an executor in life. And often they blame other people for their lack of success. Work off the karma through good deeds, volunteering, and charity.
11:
● People who have two ones in their psychomatrix are distinguished by a soft character; they love praise very much and earn it in every possible way. That is precisely why they help others a great deal, easily fit into any company and are noticeable in it, because they strive to draw attention to themselves so as to later receive gratitude for a pleasant encounter. They know how to listen to another person, as long as that person does not try to pressure them. They very rarely use pressure on another person. They often lack firmness in asserting their views, and they strive for a profession that does not depend on anyone. Quite often they lack determination and self-esteem, which lowers their goals.
● If they are fortunate and their psychomatrix contains "22" or "2222 and more," their initiative sharply activates. Such people are motivated by praise directed at them. It is important to emphasize their significance — then they achieve great success.
● If a child has character "11," when performing a task of any kind, be it homework or household chores, know that, having completed one task, the child will wait for praise for it and only then continue the list, receiving praise for each item separately.
■ It is best to use the following plan:
■ Tell the child that they must do one single task, because it turns out better for them than for others
■ After which tell the child about the chores you will take on yourself, and list them.
■ When the parents return home, they will be pleasantly surprised by a list of several tasks the child completed in order to receive an extra portion of praise; then there is no need to scold the child if they did not manage to finish the assigned task.
■ That is precisely why you should give the child as a mandatory task something that can be done tomorrow. Praise more often — and parents will have no problems with the child. You should not over-praise if the child has 88 or 55, as they cannot tolerate falsehood, because they are a "truth-seeker."
"11" is the ideal Eastern wife, Eastern submissiveness; these people are soft and flexible but can bend under a higher-standing authority. They often doubt their own abilities, heed others' opinions, and lack confidence.
Also, "11" is an indicator of a soft character. There is a character, generally speaking, but it is soft and not proactive. The character is calm, the person easily comes to agreement with others. Such people listen to others and do not pressure them. This is the most comfortable type of character for working with people; such people can not only listen but also hear, and it is very easy and pleasant to communicate with them. They are always courteous and tactful in their expressions. They very rarely use pressure on another person.
 But they lack the firmness to assert their own interests. The biggest downside of such people is their inability to say no, and those around them know this and many take advantage of it.
This variant is normal and comfortable for women. For men, of course, one would wish for a bit more firmness and confidence.
They have no innate strength of will, and therefore need constant external support (status, medals, certificates). They love to emphasize their status and significance through external attributes (big cars, expensive accessories).
11 — acquired karma from the mother's lineage. (Engaging in charity, honoring one's lineage, etc.)
Recommendations: work with your boundaries, learn to assert them, learn to say no.
Notable examples: Lyaysan Utyasheva, Kristina Asmus, Timati, Marilyn Monroe.
111:
This character bears the rather loud name "the golden mean," which immediately sets it apart from other characters. Such people can find common ground with anyone. The point is that, when necessary, they can yield in an argument to anyone (even a "1"). They can be soft and flexible, but if someone encroaches on their boundaries, they can firmly defend them. The main thing to know is that you must not pressure such a person, otherwise they will dig in, or do something out of spite, do the opposite, or not do anything at all. Also, under pressure, such a person may act deliberately or out of spite, or do the opposite. The manifestation of the character is impulsive — under pressure the character strongly reveals itself and the person can fight back.
These people are very unpredictable, impulsive, and contradictory by their nature. Sharp mood swings are typical. They themselves do not know what they want, and sometimes this trait is very puzzling to those around them. But it is always wildly interesting to be with them, because you never know what they will pull off next.
• "111" The golden mean within a person — two energies are present (1 — water — flexibility, softness; 2 — fire — firmness, decisiveness), an ideal combination for a woman, while men are advised to show more firmness. A person with the "golden mean" character makes a transition that is natural for them into a new character: "1 and 8." It corresponds to a soft and tolerant character.
This outwardly deceptive softness tempts those who love to dominate others to test their power on people who have the "111" character, which turns into an unexpected burst of emotions, and this leads to the suppression of the despot. Unpredictable in their character. Excellent actors and performers. These people need to choose a public profession. The most fortunate combination.
Notable examples: Alla Pugacheva, Princess Diana.
1111:
The character of a leader — strong-willed, powerful. Self-sufficient, a leader, demanding of others and of themselves. The person has a great deal of psychic energy (a firm inner core); whatever happens, no adversity can break this person.
These people know how to set goals for themselves; they do not like to yield in arguments, but they also do not cross certain boundaries, because they have no despotism and always remember their interlocutor, never seeking to offend them.
Never forget that this is the character of a leader, even if for a time this person becomes obedient (the vivid leadership qualities will still manifest over time). Do not try to humiliate someone with "1111" — it will end in a harsh rebuff on their part. Such people can make a decision at once and rarely give it up.
Stubborn, confident, purposeful.
Since they possess a strong inner core, they do not like to show their problems to those around them, preferring to solve their problems themselves.
They do not like displays of weakness of character in others, just as they themselves do not like to show weakness.
They need to learn to hear other people.
For a woman, such a character is sometimes expressed in problems in her personal life, because she loves to compete and to hold the leading position in relationships.
▪︎For a man, such a character is most favorable — strong, confident, strong-willed, an alpha male; it is considered a strong masculine willful character. These people need to learn to hear others; such a character best suits managers or the military. Never forget that this is the character of a leader who wishes to be better than others.
▪︎For a woman, such a character may give rise to problems in her personal life, or a soft husband by her side.
Do not think they are always assertive, because in a calm state they very much resemble those with "11" — they too love praise, but they cannot tolerate flattery and ingratiation. It is advisable to develop one's personality, so as not to later look for those to blame for one's falling behind. Such a character absolutely must reveal their talent and not be lazy. Otherwise they may later regret their idleness, because the character of a leader strives to be a manager. They have a firm will and a strong character. They will achieve everything they want. They have an explosive character and can wound. As a rule, several marriages.
Notable examples: Vladimir Putin, Svetlana Loboda, Nastasya Samburskaya, Sergei Bezrukov, Konstantin Khabensky.
11111:
The most maximally expressed character of power. The person is marked by authoritarianism and despotism. Such people pay little attention to those around them. In their career and business they are loners, because it is hard for them to work in a team. Or they are unequivocally a leader. Such people have many ideas, desires, and goals. They rarely listen to the advice of others. Very ambitious, strong-willed.
The person advances toward their goal, paying no attention to the people standing nearby. Such people are justly called despots; unlimited power — or no power at all. Rarely can any of them stop; there is no limit to their power-hungry aspirations. They strive to have everything and all at once.
Such people are obsessed with control; they strive to control their life in all spheres, as well as the lives of those close to them. A hot-tempered and dynamic character. They are terrifying in anger. In life they may behave like a 111, and only in a critical situation do they reveal their harsh character. People with such numbers must not be angered. These people are distinguished by stubbornness, confidence in themselves and in their own rightness, often arrogance and lust for power. Often these qualities begin to manifest in those with "11111" already from childhood. Children may show their character and try to manage their parents. They decide such questions as: the addition of a little sister or brother to the family. They always stand their ground, and it is very hard to dissuade them from a decision they have made, especially through criticism or coercion — this will only provoke aggression and even greater stubbornness. At the same time, such a person, as a rule, is not afraid of conflicts, even if the opponent surpasses them in strength, experience, age, or any other parameter. But do not think these qualities will manifest every day — in the absence of external pressure or stress, such a person can be soft, friendly, and accommodating. For example, they may not care which film to see with friends or what food to order at a café. But the moment it comes to matters and values that are a matter of principle for them, they will stand their ground. 111 11 — there is a downside: tyrants, willful self-styled despots with a strong character and natural magnetism. If they were not suppressed in childhood, they are natural-born leaders. With these people one must not argue. It provokes aggression and the destruction of relationships (the character of a neurotic).
Notable examples: Demi Moore, Leonardo DiCaprio, Donald Trump.
111111:
An "overload" of the despot, which means a sharp change of character — that is, a very strong, domineering person can slide into being soft, obedient, weak-willed, and drifting with the current.
They are very tolerant and calm; bursts of emotion happen only after a long accumulation of grievances. But if such a moment arrives, they are truly terrifying in anger; in such moments they are marked by fury and cruelty.
The purposefulness of such people is greatly inflated, as is their self-esteem. Character 111 111 — very similar to the character, but loves to control everything that concerns them. Loves praise very much, often takes offense. One must learn to reveal one's talent.
In life they look like an 11 and like a 1 — much ambition, but they often do not know how to express it; in critical situations they reveal their character (alcohol must not be consumed). Loves to control everything that concerns them. Loves praise very much, often takes offense. One must learn to reveal one's talent.
1111111: Such people usually come to change the world; within them is laid genius or a special talent or gift that they must realize. A rare character. Their behavior can be calm; they do not wish to take much upon themselves. They avoid responsibility. They do not let people get especially close, creating their own rules by which they strive to subordinate everyone. Prestige is one of the main aspects in realizing themselves. They live by their own ideals. They have their own truth about everything. If they fall under someone else's influence, they may go to extremes and even to murder (jealous lovers). Everyone is just an ordinary person, but I am the most important. Commanders in life. They look down on everything and thereby earn negative karma.
